input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
# .NET MAUI Development Setup on macOS

## 1. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode)
- After install, run: `xcode-select --install` (command-line tools)
- Accept license: `sudo xcodeLicense --accept`
- Open Xcode once to install additional components

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest .NET 8+ SDK (MAUI ships with it)
- Or use Homebrew: `brew install dotnet-sdk`
- Discover versions: `dotnet --list-sdks`

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```

- List available workloads: `dotnet workload search`
- Verify installed: `dotnet workload list`

## 4. Install Android SDK & JDK

The MAUI workload installs Android dependencies automatically, but to manage them:

- **Android SDK:** Installed via the workload under `~/Library/Developer/Xamarin/android-sdk-macosx` or set `ANDROID_HOME`
- **JDK:** Microsoft's OpenJDK is bundled. Alternatively install via Homebrew: `brew install microsoft-openjdk`
- To manually manage: install **Android Studio** from [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio), then use SDK Manager for specific API levels

## 5. (Optional) Install Visual Studio Code or Visual Studio for Mac's successor

- **VS Code** + C# Dev Kit extension: [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com)
  - Extensions: `C# Dev Kit`, `.NET MAUI` extension
- Or use **JetBrains Rider** with MAUI support

## 6. Verify the Installation

```bash
# Check .NET
dotnet --version

# Check workloads
dotnet workload list
# Should show: maui, maui-ios, maui-android, etc.

# Create and build a test project
dotnet new maui -n TestApp
cd TestApp
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios
dotnet build -f net8.0-android

# Run on iOS simulator
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-ios

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -t:Run -f net8.0-android
```

## 7. Version Discovery Tips

| Component | How to check latest |
|-----------|-------------------|
| .NET SDK | `dotnet --list-sdks` or [dot.net](https://dot.net) |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload update` |
| Xcode | App Store / `xcodebuild -version` |
| Android API | Android Studio SDK Manager |

## Key Environment Variables

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
export JAVA_HOME="/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/microsoft-*.jdk/Contents/Home"
export PATH="$ANDROID_HOME/emulator:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$PATH"
```

## Common Gotchas

- **Xcode version must match Apple's iOS SDK requirements** — check [Microsoft's support matrix](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/supported-platforms)
- Run `dotnet workload repair` if builds fail after SDK updates
- iOS physical device deployment requires an Apple Developer account ($99/year)
